video:video.video:title: A US strike on a suspected drug boat off Costa Rica leaves 2 dead, 1 critically hurt
video:video.video:description: A U.S. strike on a boat suspected of transporting illegal drugs along Costa Rica's Pacific coast left at least two people dead and one seriously injured, Costa Rican authorities said Friday.

video:video.video:title: Flowers are placed at Chuck Norris' Walk of Fame star
video:video.video:description: Flowers are placed at Chuck Norris’ Walk of Fame star after the actor and martial artist died Thursday at 86. (March 20)

video:video.video:title: Gwyneth Paltrow honored at annual New York Women in Film & Television Muse Awards
video:video.video:description: At the New York Women in Film & Television Muse Awards held Friday in New York City, honoree Gwyneth Paltrow says it's important for women to come together and support one another.

video:video.video:title: Iranian family in Indiana celebrates Nowruz, worries about loved ones in Iran
video:video.video:description: Nahid Rezaei and her husband, Farman Moayed, are celebrating Nowruz in their home in a suburb of Indianapolis.

video:video.video:title: Thousands told to evacuate flooding in Hawaii as officials warn dam could fail
video:video.video:description: Muddy floodwaters from severe rains have inundated streets, swallowed vehicles and prompted evacuation orders for more than 4,000 people in towns north of Honolulu.

video:video.video:title: Protesters in Slovenia unsuccessfully approach US embassy, urge voting in parliamentary elections
video:video.video:description: Left-wing protesters gathered in the Slovenian capital Ljubljana on Friday, calling on voters to take part in Sunday’s parliamentary elections and warning against foreign interference in the campaign.

video:video.video:title: Eid treats bring comfort to children and families displaced by conflict in Lebanon
video:video.video:description: Volunteers gathered to prepare food parcels and Eid sweets for children and families displaced by missile strikes on their Lebanese homes.

video:video.video:title: Beautiful hidden cloister once a centre of Rome's religious intrigue
video:video.video:description: In the heart of Rome, just a stone’s throw from the Pantheon, there is a fascinating place that is normally closed to the public: the marvelous Guidetti Cloister of the Basilica of Santa Maria sopra Minerva.

video:video.video:title: Endangered monkey gives birth after pioneering surgery
video:video.video:description: A critically endangered roloway monkey has given birth at Chester Zoo, months after a complex operation saved her foot from amputation.

video:video.video:title: Body of American student who went missing in Barcelona has been found
video:video.video:description: The body of James “Jimmy” Gracey, a college student who went missing while in Spain on spring break, was found Thursday in the water off a Barcelona beach, police said.

video:video.video:title: Strong rains flood homes in Colombia
video:video.video:description: Emergency workers on Friday were assisting residents in Facatativá, on the outskirts of the Colombian capital, who saw their homes and town flooded following strong rains in recent days.

video:video.video:title: Robert Kraft and Michael Rubin support as Tom Brady gears up to return to the field
video:video.video:description: New England Patriots owner Robert Kraft and Fanatics founder Michael Rubin pose with Tom Brady as he prepares to hit the field for the first time since 2023.

video:video.video:title: Worshippers gather in Kyiv to pay respects to the late Patriarch Filaret
video:video.video:description: Worshippers in Kyiv gathered at St. Michael's Golden-Domed Monastery on Friday to pay their respects to the late Patriarch Emeritus Filaret, one of the leaders of the Orthodox Church of Ukraine.

video:video.video:title: Kurdish Nowruz festival lights up Akra with torches and fireworks
video:video.video:description: Thousands of Kurds celebrated the new year festival of Nowruz in the hub city of Akra in Iraq's autonomous Kurdish region.

video:video.video:title: Iraqi Kurds celebrate Nowruz amid security concerns and Iran war - AP explains
video:video.video:description: Thousands of Kurds celebrated the new year festival of Nowruz in the hub city of Akra in Iraq's autonomous Kurdish region.

video:video.video:title: Tons of aid flow into Cuba as humanitarian convoy arrives on the struggling island
video:video.video:description: Some 650 delegates from 33 countries and 120 organizations began arriving in Cuba on Friday as part of a solidarity caravan transporting some 20 tons of humanitarian aid as the island grapples with a severe energy crisis.

video:video.video:title: Gwyneth Paltrow talks supporting women
video:video.video:description: Gwyneth Paltrow talks about the importance of supporting women at the New York Women in Film and Television Muse Awards.

video:video.video:title: DEA names Colombian president ‘priority target’ as US prosecutors probe ties to drug traffickers
video:video.video:description: Colombian President Gustavo Petro has been designated a “priority target” by the U.S. Drug Enforcement Administration as federal prosecutors in New York probe his alleged ties to drug traffickers, according to people familiar with the matter and records seen by The Associated Press.

video:video.video:title: Police video of Justin Timberlake's 2024 drunken driving arrest released
video:video.video:description: Video of recording artist Justin Timberlake was released Friday by Sag Harbor Police after the village and Timberlake’s lawyers agreed to disclose a redacted version of the footage.

video:video.video:title: Women-only gyms challenge toxic masculinity
video:video.video:description: Women are taking over some of the heavy lifting from the “gym bros” with women-only gyms designed to combat toxic masculinity.

video:video.video:title: Jury finds Elon Musk misled investors during Twitter purchase, AP explains
video:video.video:description: A jury has found Elon Musk liable for defrauding investors by deliberately driving down Twitter's stock price in the tumultuous months leading up to his 2022 acquisition of the social media company for $44 billion.

video:video.video:title: Argentinian winemakers move away from chemicals towards organic wine
video:video.video:description: The grape harvest is underway in the Mendoza region of Argentina. In the Uco Valley, some producers have chosen to grow their grapes in closer harmony with the natural environment to encourage biodiversity.

video:video.video:title: Thousands told to evacuate flooding in Oahu's North Shore as heavy rains fall in Hawaii, AP explains
video:video.video:description: Muddy floodwaters from severe rains have inundated communities and prompted evacuation orders for more than 5,500 people in towns north of Honolulu.

video:video.video:title: Worshippers hope for peace as they mark Eid al-Fitr with prayers across Asia
video:video.video:description: Muslims across the world gathered at mosques on Saturday for Eid prayers. Eid is typically greeted with joy and excitement and is marked with congregational prayers and festivities that usually include family visits, gatherings, outings and new clothes.

video:video.video:title: Jack Quaid on 'The Boys' final season: 'No character is safe'
video:video.video:description: "The Boys" stars, including Jack Quaid, Antony Starr and Karl Urban, talked about the surprises in the final season of Prime Video’s superhero satire series, at the show's world premiere in Rome.

video:video.video:title: Fire at South Korean auto parts factory injures over 50
video:video.video:description: South Korean officials say at least 55 people were injured in a fire at an auto parts factory in the central city of Daejeon.

video:video.video:title: Chuck Norris dies at 86
video:video.video:description: Chuck Norris, the martial arts grandmaster and action star whose roles in “Walker, Texas Ranger” and other television shows and movies made him an iconic tough guy — sparking internet parodies and adoration from presidents — has died at 86.

video:video.video:title: Trump administration sues Harvard, saying it violated civil rights law
video:video.video:description: The Justice Department filed a new lawsuit Friday against Harvard University, saying its leadership failed to address antisemitism on campus, creating grounds for the government to freeze existing grants and seek repayment for grants already paid.

video:video.video:title: Tips to stay safe of tax season scams
video:video.video:description: Robocalls, texts, and phishing emails from scammers are up this tax season compared to previous years, with artificial intelligence likely increasing fraud attempts, according to the consumer protection bureau of the Federal Trade Commission.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories March 20
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Friday, March 20th: U.S. sending more warships, 2,500 Marines to Middle East; Dignified transfer honors U.S. soldier killed in Iran war; NASA rocket rolled back out to launch pad for Artemis II mission; Stocks fall again

video:video.video:title: The faces of BTS light up night sky in Seoul, in drone display
video:video.video:description: Thousands of drones create portraits of the seven members of BTS, in a light show watched by hundreds of fans on the banks of the river Han, Friday night, ahead of the K-pop superstars' hotly anticipated comeback concert.

video:video.video:title: A global party begins: BTS relaunches with a massive Seoul concert
video:video.video:description: BTS will stage its long-awaited comeback concert on Saturday night in Seoul. In a free concert expected to draw tens of thousands of fans, the K-pop juggernaut's seven members will perform songs from their first album in nearly four years, “ARIRANG.”

video:video.video:title: Iran threatens world tourism sites and says it's still building missiles - AP explains
video:video.video:description: Iran threatened to target recreational and tourist sites worldwide and insisted it was still building missiles.

video:video.video:title: Mood is ‘very gloomy’ as Michigan Muslims mark Eid al-Fitr holiday amid war
video:video.video:description: Michigan Muslims in the capital of Arab America welcomed the holiday of Eid al-Fitr with decidedly mixed feelings. (AP video: Mike Householder)

video:video.video:title: Army Staff Sgt. killed in war with Iran returns home to Kentucky in dignified transfer
video:video.video:description: The body of Army Staff Sgt. Benjamin N. Pennington, the seventh U.S. service member to die in combat during the U.S. war with Iran, was returned home Friday in preparation for his funeral.

video:video.video:title: Worshippers pray outside Jerusalem’s Old City walls to mark Eid al-Fitr
video:video.video:description: Worshippers prayed outside Jerusalem’s Old City walls as they observed Eid al-Fitr prayers on Friday.

video:video.video:title: BTS fans share their expectations for Gwanghwamun comeback concert
video:video.video:description: With the BTS comeback concert in Seoul a day away, fans from all over the world gathered in Yeouido for a fan event to celebrate the K-pop hitmakers' long-awaited comeback.

video:video.video:title: Heat wave in southwest US forecast to continue through weekend
video:video.video:description: The record-setting heatwave in the southwestern U.S. is forecast to continue into Sunday. A desert community in southwestern Arizona reached 110 degrees on Thursday, breaking a record for the highest March temperature recorded in the United States.

video:video.video:title: Kim Jong Un sits on a tank with his daughter at a military exercise
video:video.video:description: In their latest joint appearance, North Korean leader Kim Jong Un and his teenage daughter rode a tank together, state media video showed Friday, days after they watched rocket launches and fired pistols.

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories March 20
video:video.video:description: Here’s the latest for Friday March 20th: Iranian attacks reported in Dubai, Kuwait, Saudi Arabia; Netanyahu says Israel won’t attack Iranian part of gas field; Trump meets with Japanese Prime Minister; Temperature record breaks during Southwest heat wave.

video:video.video:title: 'Buffy' star's message to fans over reboot cancellation
video:video.video:description: At a special event in London for her latest film "Ready or Not: Here I Come," Sarah Michelle Gellar says she's been moved by fans' reaction to the cancellation of a "Buffy the Vampire Slayer" reboot.

video:video.video:title: Aftermath of strike on checkpoint in Tehran as Israel continues attacks
video:video.video:description: The aftermath of a strike on a checkpoint in Tehran was captured on video Tuesday as Israel and the United States continued their attacks in Iran.

video:video.video:title: An exiled Iranian women's soccer player understands the struggles of team in Australia seeking asylum
video:video.video:description: Shiva Amini, a exiled player for the Iranian women’s national soccer team now living in New York City, says she’s been in touch with a some of the current members of the team, including some who decided to return home rather than seek asylum in Australia.

video:video.video:title: Heat records shattered across West as climate change impacts intensify
video:video.video:description: Scientists say a record March heat wave in the U.S. West shows climate change is already driving more dangerous weather extremes.

video:video.video:title: Palestinians mark the start of Eid al-Fitr with prayers in Gaza City’s Saraya yard
video:video.video:description: With much of Gaza in rubble, Palestinians marked the start of Eid al-Fitr with prayers in Gaza City’s Saraya yard, where Eid prayers were held for the first time since the war started in 2023.

video:video.video:title: Debris of an Iranian missile hit the Old City in Jerusalem
video:video.video:description: A strike hit near Jerusalem’s Old City on Friday, close to the hilltop compound home to religious sites revered by Jews and Muslims, marking the danger facing holy sites.

video:video.video:title: How Netflix is bringing BTS's Gwanghwamun comeback to the world
video:video.video:description: Netflix is pulling out all the stops for BTS’s comeback, deploying more than 1,000 crew members and 25 cameras - including rare government approved drones - to broadcast the show live from Seoul’s historic Gwanghwamun Square. (20 March)

video:video.video:title: Funeral held in Tehran for Iran's intelligence minister and his family
video:video.video:description: A funeral was held in Tehran for Iran's slain intelligence minister and his family on Friday.

video:video.video:title: Long security lines at Houston airport as partial government shutdown continues
video:video.video:description: The turmoil at security and customs lanes is tied to a partial government shutdown that began February 14 after Democrats and the White House were unable to reach a deal on legislation to fund the Department of Homeland Security.

video:video.video:title: NASA hauls its repaired moon rocket from the hangar back to the pad for an early April launch
video:video.video:description: The NASA Artemis II rocket with the Orion spacecraft moved toward the pad Friday following repairs Vehicle Assembly Building.

video:video.video:title: Uganda reintroduces rhinos into protected area where they've been extinct since 1983
video:video.video:description: Ugandan wildlife authorities have reintroduced rhinos into a remote protected area where they were once poached into extinction, an event seen by conservationists as a milestone in efforts to support the recovery of a species threatened by poaching.

video:video.video:title: Valencia burns effigies as Las Fallas fire and firework festival comes to an end
video:video.video:description: Hundreds of painstakingly crafted wood and papier-maché sculptures were burned during the festival of Las Fallas, the most important yearly celebration in Valencia, on Thursday.

video:video.video:title: Are poodle mixed breed dogs really too hard to handle?
video:video.video:description: Mixed-breed dogs are more likely to show problem behaviour, according to a new study by a leading veterinary organisation.

video:video.video:title: British lawmaker and Irish hip-hop trio Kneecap in Cuba as humanitarian convoy arrives on the island
video:video.video:description: British lawmaker and former Labour party leader Jeremy Corbyn called on nations to "stand up" to the U.S. over its continued blockade of Cuba.

video:video.video:title: Officials say at least 10 people were killed in a South Korean factory fire
video:video.video:description: South Korean rescue workers on Saturday recovered the remains of 10 people from the charred wreckage of an auto parts factory in the central city of Daejeon, where a blaze likely triggered by an explosion injured at least 59 others and left four missing.

video:video.video:title: Syrian Kurds return to Afrin, celebrate Nowruz for first time since exile and official recognition
video:video.video:description: Syrian Kurds have returned to Afrin to celebrate Nowruz for the first time after their exile, and for the first time after a government decree marked the celebration as a national holiday.

video:video.video:title: Associated Press journalist documents her journey to the BTS reunion show
video:video.video:description: Associated Press journalist Juwon Park documents her journey to BTS' first show in Seoul after 4-year hiatus. (Mar. 21)

video:video.video:title: Israel says fragments of Iranian missile struck a kindergarten in centre of country
video:video.video:description: The Israeli military said Saturday that fragments of an Iranian missile struck a kindergarten in the central Israeli city of Rishon Lezion, causing damage.

video:video.video:title: Dozens of models with Downs syndrome take to the catwalk at Bucharest fashion show
video:video.video:description: Dozens of models with Downs syndrome strutted down a catwalk at a fashion show in Romania’s capital for an evening celebrating style, “atypical beauty” and courage to mark World Downs Syndrome Day.

video:video.video:title: Defense Minister Katz says Israel and the US will step up attacks on Iran
video:video.video:description: Israel’s defense minister said Saturday that joint U.S.-Israeli military operations against Iran will intensify in the coming week.

video:video.video:title: US-Israeli strikes severely damage hospital in Iran's Khuzestan province
video:video.video:description: A hospital and tourist site in southwestern Iran have been damaged from U.S. or Israeli strikes, killing at least one child, according to Iranian news agencies.

video:video.video:title: New photos of Neil Armstrong from the Gemini 8 mission released
video:video.video:description: Newly released photographs are shedding light on a little known but critical moment in Neil Armstrong’s career, years before he became the first person to walk on the moon.

video:video.video:title: Former FBI Director Robert Mueller, who investigated Russia-Trump campaign ties, dies
video:video.video:description: Robert S. Mueller III, the FBI director who later became special counsel in charge of investigating ties between Russia and Donald Trump’s presidential campaign, has died.

video:video.video:title: 20 people reported injured in the southern Israeli city of Dimona following Iranian missile hits
video:video.video:description: Magen David Adom Israel reported 20 people injured in the Southern city of Dimona following several Iranian missile hits on Saturday evening.

video:video.video:title: Egypt's President meets King Hamad bin Isa Al Khalifa in Manama during Gulf tour
video:video.video:description: Egypt's President Abdel-Fattah el-Sissi on Saturday met with the King of Bahrain in Manama to discuss the escalating conflict in the region.

video:video.video:title: For airline travelers, the shutdown answer is simple: Pay TSA officers
video:video.video:description: Regardless of politics or destination, American air travelers were unified by one desire Saturday: It's time to pay Transportation Security Administration employees.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories March 21
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Friday, March 21st: Trump demands Democrats fund the Department of Homeland Security; Flash flood in Hawaii forces thousands to evacuate; Muslims celebrate Eid al-Fitr around the world; Former FBI Director Robert Mueller dies.

video:video.video:title: Volunteers mobilize for World Water Day cleanup in Rio de Janeiro
video:video.video:description: Ahead of World Water Day, volunteers gathered across the iconic Guanabara Bay for a CleanUp Bay effort, taking place simultaneously across five municipalities and bringing together environmental groups and local communities.

video:video.video:title: Cuba’s power grid collapses leaving it without electricity for the 3rd time this month
video:video.video:description: Cuba’s power grid collapsed Saturday leaving the country without electricity for a third time in March as the communist government battles with a decaying infrastructure and a U.S.

video:video.video:title: Airports set up donation boxes for TSA workers amid government shutdown
video:video.video:description: TSA agents are facing financial pressures during an ongoing government funding lapse — the third shutdown in fewer than six months that has forced the officers who screen airport passengers and luggage to keep working without pay.

video:video.video:title: Tens of thousands of protesters rally in Prague against new government of Czech prime minister Babiš
video:video.video:description: Tens of thousands of people protested in Prague on Saturday against the new Czech government led by populist Prime Minister Andrej Babiš.

video:video.video:title: Hawaii’s worst flooding in 20 years threatens dam, prompts evacuations as more rain looms
video:video.video:description: As Hawaii endures its worst flooding in more than 20 years, officials are urging people in hard-hit areas to evacuate.

video:video.video:title: America's official mammal, the bison, gets a bronze tribute for the country's 250th birthday
video:video.video:description: Three bison statues cast in bronze have taken up a permanent display outside the Smithsonian’s National Museum of Natural History in Washington.

video:video.video:title: Dozens injured in Israel after Iranian missile strikes target areas near nuclear research center
video:video.video:description: Iran struck two communities near Israel’s main nuclear research center late Saturday, leaving buildings shattered and at least seven people seriously injured, hours after Tehran's main nuclear enrichment site was hit as the war spun into a dangerous new direction at the start of its fourth week.
